theory.title: Speaking Skills: Describing People and Their Achievements

sections.heading: 1. Tóm tắt lý thuyết
sections.content:
🗣️ Kỹ năng nói về người bạn ngưỡng mộ

Cấu trúc bài nói:

  • Introduction: Giới thiệu người đó là ai
  • Background: Thông tin cơ bản (sinh năm nào, quê ở đâu)
  • Achievements: Thành tựu, đóng góp chính
  • Qualities: Phẩm chất đáng ngưỡng mộ
  • Personal connection: Tại sao bạn ngưỡng mộ họ
  • Conclusion: Kết luận, ảnh hưởng của họ

Useful expressions:

  • I really admire... / I look up to...
  • He/She is known for...
  • What I admire most about him/her is...
  • He/She has made significant contributions to...
  • His/Her story inspires me because...
  • He/She overcame many challenges such as...

sections.heading: 2. Useful Vocabulary and Phrases
sections.content:
FunctionExpressionsExample
IntroducingThe person I admire most is...
I'd like to talk about...
Let me tell you about...		The person I admire most is my grandmother.
Describing qualitiesHe/She is incredibly...
What makes him/her special is...
He/She possesses...		She is incredibly determined and hardworking.
Talking about achievementsHe/She achieved...
He/She is famous for...
He/She succeeded in...		She achieved remarkable success in her field.
Expressing admirationI really look up to...
I'm inspired by...
What I admire most is...		What I admire most is her perseverance.
Giving reasonsThe reason why...
This is because...
I admire him/her because...		I admire her because she never gave up.
ConcludingIn conclusion...
Overall...
His/Her legacy...		Overall, she has had a huge impact on my life.

sections.heading: 3. Năm ví dụ mẫu về Speaking
sections.content:
Example 1: Talking about a family member

"The person I admire most is my grandmother. She's 75 years old and has lived through many difficult times. What I admire most about her is her strength and kindness. Despite facing poverty when she was young, she worked hard to raise five children and give them a good education. She always helps others in our community and never complains. Her positive attitude inspires me to be grateful for what I have and to help others whenever I can."

Example 2: Talking about a historical figure

"I'd like to talk about Nelson Mandela, a South African leader who fought against apartheid. He spent 27 years in prison for his beliefs but never gave up. What makes him truly admirable is his ability to forgive. After becoming president, he promoted reconciliation instead of revenge. His famous quote 'Education is the most powerful weapon' has inspired me to value my education and use it to make a positive difference in society."

Example 3: Talking about a teacher

"My English teacher, Ms. Lan, is someone I really look up to. She's been teaching for 20 years and is incredibly passionate about her work. What I admire about her is how she makes every student feel valued. She stays after class to help struggling students and always encourages us to believe in ourselves. Thanks to her dedication, I've become more confident in speaking English. She's shown me that a good teacher can change lives."

Example 4: Talking about a scientist

"Marie Curie is a scientist I greatly admire. She was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize and the only person to win Nobel Prizes in two different sciences. Despite facing discrimination as a woman in science, she persevered and made groundbreaking discoveries in radioactivity. What inspires me most is her dedication to knowledge. She worked in dangerous conditions and eventually died from radiation exposure. Her sacrifice advanced science and opened doors for women in STEM fields."

Example 5: Talking about an activist

"Malala Yousafzai is a young activist I deeply admire. At just 15, she survived an assassination attempt because she spoke out for girls' education. Instead of giving up, she became even more determined. She co-founded the Malala Fund and won the Nobel Peace Prize at 17. What I find most inspiring is her courage and her belief that education can change the world. Her story teaches me that age doesn't matter when you're fighting for what's right."

sections.heading: 4. Các dạng bài tập Speaking
sections.content:
📝 Dạng bài tập Speaking thường gặp

Dạng 1: Monologue (Nói một mình)

Nói về một chủ đề trong 1-2 phút không bị gián đoạn.

Tip: Chuẩn bị outline với các ý chính, luyện tập trước.

Dạng 2: Dialogue (Đối thoại)

Trò chuyện với bạn hoặc giáo viên về chủ đề.

Tip: Lắng nghe câu hỏi, trả lời đầy đủ, hỏi lại để duy trì cuộc trò chuyện.

Dạng 3: Picture Description

Mô tả hình ảnh về một người nổi tiếng.

Tip: Mô tả từ tổng quan đến chi tiết, sử dụng từ vựng phong phú.

Dạng 4: Role-play

Đóng vai phỏng vấn người nổi tiếng hoặc giới thiệu họ.

Tip: Chuẩn bị câu hỏi và câu trả lời, thể hiện tự nhiên.

Dạng 5: Discussion

Thảo luận nhóm về người đáng ngưỡng mộ.

Tip: Đưa ra ý kiến, lắng nghe người khác, đồng ý/không đồng ý lịch sự.

exercises.question: Which expression is best for introducing the person you admire?
options: The person I admire most is
exercises.explanation: 'The person I admire most is...' là cách giới thiệu phù hợp nhất.

exercises.question: Complete: 'What I admire most about her is her _____.'
exercises.explanation: 'Determination' (sự quyết tâm) là phẩm chất đáng ngưỡng mộ.

exercises.question: Which phrase expresses admiration correctly?
options: I really look up to her
exercises.explanation: 'Look up to' nghĩa là ngưỡng mộ, tôn trọng ai đó.

exercises.question: Choose the best way to describe achievements: 'She _____ remarkable success in science.'
exercises.explanation: 'Achieved' (đạt được) là động từ phù hợp với 'success'.

exercises.question: Which expression is used to give reasons?
exercises.explanation: 'I admire her because...' là cách đưa ra lý do rõ ràng.

exercises.question: Complete: 'His story _____ me to work harder.'
exercises.explanation: 'Inspires' (truyền cảm hứng) phù hợp với ngữ cảnh tích cực.

exercises.question: Which phrase describes overcoming challenges?
exercises.explanation: 'Overcame many obstacles' nghĩa là vượt qua nhiều trở ngại.

exercises.question: Choose the correct conclusion: '_____, she has made a huge impact on society.'
exercises.explanation: 'In conclusion' là cách kết luận phù hợp.

exercises.question: Which quality is most admirable?
exercises.explanation: 'Perseverance' (sự kiên trì) là phẩm chất đáng ngưỡng mộ.

exercises.question: Complete: 'She is _____ for her contributions to education.'
exercises.explanation: 'Famous for' nghĩa là nổi tiếng về điều gì.

exercises.question_text: Decide if these speaking strategies are good (True) or bad (False):
options: Provide examples to support your points.
exercises.explanation: a) False - Nên nói với tốc độ vừa phải và có pauses. b) True - Linking words giúp bài nói mạch lạc. c) False - Nên trả lời đầy đủ. d) True - Ví dụ làm bài nói sinh động hơn.

exercises.question_text: Are these expressions appropriate for describing admirable people? Mark True or False:
options: 'I really look up to her dedication.'
exercises.explanation: a) False - Đây là phẩm chất tiêu cực. b) True - Mô tả phẩm chất tích cực. c) True - Thể hiện sự ngưỡng mộ. d) False - Không phù hợp khi nói về người ngưỡng mộ.

exercises.question_text:
Read this speaking sample and decide if the statements are True (T) or False (F):

'I admire my mother because she is hardworking and caring. She wakes up early every day to prepare breakfast for the family. Despite having a full-time job, she always finds time to help me with my homework.'
options: The speaker explains why they admire their mother.
The speaker describes qualities and actions.
exercises.explanation: a) True - Có ví dụ cụ thể (dậy sớm, giúp bài tập). b) True - Giải thích lý do (hardworking, caring). c) False - Ngôn ngữ tích cực. d) True - Mô tả cả phẩm chất và hành động.

exercises.passage:
Speaking Task: You have 2 minutes to talk about a person you admire. Here are some prompts to help you:

• Who is this person?
• How do you know them?
• What are their main achievements or qualities?
• Why do you admire them?
• How have they influenced you?

Sample Answer:
'I'd like to talk about my uncle, who is a doctor. I've known him all my life as he's my father's younger brother. What makes him admirable is his dedication to helping others. He works at a rural hospital where resources are limited, but he never complains. He often works overtime to treat patients who can't afford private healthcare. What I admire most is his compassion and selflessness. He could earn much more in a city hospital, but he chooses to serve poor communities. His example has inspired me to consider a career in medicine. He's shown me that success isn't just about money, but about making a positive difference in people's lives. I hope to be as dedicated and caring as he is.'

questions.question: What is the relationship between the speaker and the person they admire?
questions.explanation: The speaker mentions 'my uncle' and 'my father's younger brother'.

questions.question: Where does the uncle work?
questions.explanation: The text states 'He works at a rural hospital'.

questions.question: What quality does the speaker admire most?
questions.explanation: The speaker says 'What I admire most is his compassion and selflessness'.

questions.question: How has the uncle influenced the speaker?
options: To consider a career in medicine
questions.explanation: The speaker says 'His example has inspired me to consider a career in medicine'.

questions.question: What lesson did the speaker learn?
questions.explanation: The speaker learned that 'success isn't just about money, but about making a positive difference'.

exercises.passage:
Interview Practice: Student A interviews Student B about a person they admire.

Student A (Interviewer): Can you tell me about someone you admire?
Student B: Yes, I admire Malala Yousafzai, the Pakistani education activist.

Student A: What does she do?
Student B: She campaigns for girls' education rights worldwide. She co-founded the Malala Fund to support education projects.

Student A: Why do you admire her?
Student B: I admire her courage. She survived an assassination attempt at age 15 but didn't give up. She continued fighting for education and won the Nobel Peace Prize at 17.

Student A: How has she influenced you?
Student B: Her story taught me to value my education and to speak up for what I believe in. She showed that young people can make a difference.

Student A: What qualities does she have?
Student B: She's brave, determined, and compassionate. Despite facing danger, she remains committed to helping others access education.

questions.question: Who does Student B admire?
questions.explanation: Student B clearly states 'I admire Malala Yousafzai'.

questions.question: What organization did Malala co-found?
questions.explanation: Student B mentions 'She co-founded the Malala Fund'.

questions.question: How old was Malala when she won the Nobel Prize?
questions.explanation: The text states 'won the Nobel Peace Prize at 17'.

questions.question: What lesson did Student B learn from Malala?
options: To value education and speak up
questions.explanation: Student B learned 'to value my education and to speak up for what I believe in'.

questions.question: Which quality is NOT mentioned about Malala?
questions.explanation: Lazy is not mentioned; the text describes her as brave, determined, and compassionate.

exercises.question_text:
Complete the speaking outline with appropriate words:

Introduction: The person I (1) _____ most is...
Background: He/She was (2) _____ in... and is known for...
Achievements: He/She (3) _____ many great things such as...
Qualities: What I admire is his/her (4) _____ and...
Influence: His/Her story (5) _____ me to...
Conclusion: (6) _____, he/she has made a significant impact.
exercises.correct:
1. admire
2. born
3. achieved/accomplished
4. determination/courage/perseverance
5. inspires/motivates
6. In conclusion/Overall
exercises.explanation: Outline cơ bản cho bài nói về người ngưỡng mộ.

exercises.question_text:
Fill in the blanks with suitable expressions:

1. _____ talk about my grandmother. (I'd like to / I hate to)
2. She _____ many challenges in her life. (avoided / overcame)
3. What I admire most is her _____. (laziness / kindness)
4. Her story _____ me to be a better person. (discourages / inspires)
exercises.correct:
1. I'd like to
2. overcame
3. kindness
4. inspires
exercises.explanation: Chọn các cụm từ tích cực và phù hợp với ngữ cảnh ngưỡng mộ.

exercises.question_text:
Complete the sentences with appropriate linking words:
[because, however, moreover, for example, in conclusion]

1. I admire her _____ she never gives up.
2. She faced many difficulties. _____, she succeeded.
3. She is kind. _____, she is also very intelligent.
4. _____, she volunteers at the hospital every weekend.
exercises.explanation: Linking words giúp bài nói mạch lạc và logic hơn.

exercises.question_text:
Arrange these parts in the correct order for a speaking task (1-6):

___ Describe their achievements
___ Give your conclusion
___ Introduce the person
___ Explain why you admire them
___ Provide background information
___ Describe their influence on you

exercises.question:
Speaking Task: Prepare a 2-minute talk about a person you admire. Write your speaking notes (not a full essay, just key points) covering:

• Introduction: Who they are
• Background: Basic information
• Achievements: What they've done
• Qualities: Why they're admirable
• Personal connection: How they've influenced you
• Conclusion: Final thoughts

Then practice speaking from your notes.
exercises.answer_guide:
Sample Speaking Notes:

Person: My English teacher, Ms. Hoa

Intro:
- Known her for 2 years
- Most inspiring teacher I've had

Background:
- Teaching for 15 years
- Studied in UK
- Returned to teach in Vietnam

Achievements:
- Helped 100s of students pass exams
- Won 'Teacher of the Year' award
- Created free online English resources

Qualities:
- Patient and encouraging
- Passionate about teaching
- Cares about every student
- Makes learning fun

Influence on me:
- Improved my English confidence
- Inspired me to become a teacher
- Taught me that hard work pays off
- Showed me importance of helping others

Conclusion:
- She's more than a teacher - a role model
- Her dedication inspires me daily
- Hope to impact others like she has

[Practice speaking naturally from these notes, expanding each point with examples and details. Aim for 2 minutes, speaking clearly and confidently.]
exercises.explanation: Bài nói cần có cấu trúc rõ ràng, sử dụng từ vựng phong phú, đưa ra ví dụ cụ thể, và thể hiện cảm xúc chân thật. Luyện tập nhiều lần để nói tự nhiên.
